member.name: Caroline Voaden
member.synopsis: Caroline Voaden is the Liberal Democrat MP for South Devon , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Schools).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Courts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, what steps she is taking with the Crown Prosecution Service to help reduce the backlog of court cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government inherited a justice system in crisis, with a backlog of eighty thousand criminal cases in the Crown court. That is why we are investing over two and a half billion pounds in our courts. This will fund unlimited sitting days, boost legal ai...

writtenQuestions.heading: Playgrounds: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the Playground Fund will be expanded to additional local authorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The £18m Playgrounds Fund is a targeted programme to improve children’s access to safe, inclusive, high‑quality local play spaces in communities where it has fallen furthest behind. This investment in playgrounds reflects the Government’s commitment to gi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Active Travel: Business
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to encourage active travel for business use.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the third Cycling and Walking Investment Strategy, published on 12 June, the Government is projected to invest £4.5 billion up to 2030 for active travel. This includes £1.1 billion of funding for Active Travel England, of which £760 million ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Education: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of the number of pupils who have moved from independent schools to state schools since the introduction of VAT on independent school fees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: School closure information is available via the register of schools, which is accessible at: https://www.get-information-schools.service.gov.uk/.The latest school census data, as at June 2026, reveals pupil numbers remain firmly within historical patterns...

writtenQuestions.answerText: School closure information is available via the register of schools, which is accessible at: https://www.get-information-schools.service.gov.uk/.The latest school census data, as at June 2026, reveals pupil numbers remain firmly within historical patterns...

writtenQuestions.heading: South West Water: Fines
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether the financial penalty incurred by South West Water at Exeter Magistrates' Court on 2 June 2026 will be paid into the Water Restoration Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Successful projects for the Water Restoration Fund (WRF) were announced in October 2025. These projects are now in delivery and there are no plans for further application rounds. On 20 January 2026, government announced alongside the White Paper that £29 ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much has been spent by the Marine and Coastguard Agency on Coastguard Rescue Officer expenses in each of the last five financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Total expenditure by the Maritime and Coastguard Agency on reimbursement of expenses incurred by Coastguard Rescue Officers is as follows: 21/2222/2323/2424/2525/26CRO - Expenses£468,223£484,244£493,558£502,633£518,793

writtenQuestions.heading: Holiday Accommodation: Registration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what progress her Department has made on the introduction of a registration scheme for short-term lets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parliament legislated for a national registration scheme for short-term lets in the Levelling Up and Regeneration Act 2023. The government remains committed to implementing this registration scheme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Licences: Health
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of timescales for medical driving licence renewals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) has seen sustained growth in the volume and complexity of medical licence applications which has led to increased waiting times for some customers. In the interests of road safety, the DVLA must be satisfied ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Horses: Dartmoor
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to protect Dartmoor ponies in the context of the publication of stocking densities for Countryside Stewardship schemes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of Dartmoor’s pony populations. Grazing rates within the Sustainable Farming Incentive and Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier have been developed in line with evidence that moderate to low grazing can benefit biod...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Horses: Dartmoor
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the impact of grazing density calculations on Dartmoor pony populations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra has assessed the impact of including ponies in grazing density calculations under Countryside Stewardship schemes and considers that this supports the sustainability of Dartmoor pony populations. Inclusion ensures ponies are recognised within scheme...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power: Government Assistance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to support small-scale onshore wind projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important role small-scale onshore wind can play in achieving our decarbonisation goals. The Government supports small-scale onshore wind through the Smart Export Guarantee (SEG), a mechanism that ensures homes or farms with ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Slaughterhouses: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of animal welfare practices in slaughterhouses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The legislation sets out strict requirements to protect the welfare of animals when slaughtered and official veterinarians of the Food Standards Agency (FSA) are present in all approved slaughterhouses to monitor and enforce welfare requirements. When the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Financial Services and Utilities: Bereavement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what data the Government holds on the number of complaints made to financial services and utility companies relating to the handling of bereavement notifications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tell Us Once is used by public sector bodies only, and does not collect data on the number of complaints made to financial services and utility companies relating to the handling of bereavement notifications.

writtenQuestions.heading: Tell Us Once Programme: Private Sector
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the feasibility of extending the Tell Us Once service to include private sector organisations including banks, insurance companies, and utility providers, in order to redu
writtenQuestions.answerText: Discovery and analysis work on both modernising the Tell Us Once service and considering its scope is due to commence in summer 2026.
